mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous CPIAUCSL acceleration and RSAFS deceleration indicates stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive returns in 70 percent of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a late-cycle expansion phase confirms resilient manufacturing output that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 5.0 percent imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60 percent of high-rate environments, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with subsequent equity returns (correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with declining real GDP signals a sector-specific bubble decoupling from broader economic weakness, which triggers risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields positive returns in 65 percent of cases, or the combined signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth alongside falling inflation during a disinflation regime confirms supply-side efficiency gains that expand profit margins and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in 70 percent of disinflation periods, or the joint effect becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL declines exceed 0.5 percent monthly.
